behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP applies: correctness depends on what the real model chooses to include from thread context. A stubbed model can always return a fixture that appears contextual, hiding failures in real grounding behavior. Rejected Workflow because behavior text targets one model judgment (contextual referencing), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the assistant against real sampled email threads and assert the draft cites thread-specific entities/details (not just template language), using rubric-based evaluation over repeated runs to capture model variance.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS is cross-module: draft-claim extraction/normalization + KB lookup + contradiction logic handoff. No explicit REAL-INFRA requirement in behavior text, and no explicit real AI/model dependency is stated for this checker. Rejected System because behavior does not require real external contract/round-trip side effect verification. Rejected Agentic because text specifies contradiction against KB entries but does not require model-quality judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser/checker/KB modules with a local test KB and drafts containing known contradictions; verify contradiction flags and propagated reasons across module boundaries.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real generation behavior under missing-knowledge conditions (LLM-DEP). A mock response can trivially include a disclaimer and mask fabrication risk. Rejected Workflow because this behavior checks a single generation-time decision under a condition, not a required multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use real model calls with prompts where retrieval returns no relevant KB entries; assert the produced draft contains the required uncertainty disclaimer and avoids unsupported factual claims across repeated trials.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: SYSTEM TRIAD trigger (3): behavior explicitly requires proving an externally visible side effect does not occur (no send) until approval, which needs round-trip observation at the send boundary. Integration-only mocks can hide real dispatch paths (jobs/webhooks/transport wiring). Rejected Agentic because this is gating/orchestration, not model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the approval flow with real send transport/API boundary enabled in a test environment; verify no outbound message exists before approval, then verify exactly one send appears after explicit approval (round-trip readback).

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requests a full agent pipeline end-to-end with at least one real model-dependent step and sequential handoffs across stages. MULTI-STEP applies: validating chained stage correctness is beyond single-step Agentic. Rejected System as final level because the core requirement is sequence-level agent pipeline correctness, which Workflow subsumes here.
behaviors.test_description: Run complete pipeline on real email-thread fixtures: ingest thread, perform real draft generation, run factual check, and present approval artifact; assert stage outputs and handoff integrity across the full sequence in one execution.
